result:
CORS errors mean browser blocked cross-origin request — server at different origin (scheme/host/port) didn't return headers browser needs to allow it.

Common causes:
- server missing Access-Control-Allow-Origin header (or value doesn't match your page's origin)
- preflight (OPTIONS) failing — server not handling OPTIONS, or missing Access-Control-Allow-Methods / Access-Control-Allow-Headers
- sending cookies/auth with request but server lacks Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true (and ACAO can't be "*" in that case, must echo exact origin)
- custom header (Authorization, X-*) triggers preflight that server doesn't expect
- wrong port — localhost:3000 calling localhost:8000 counts as cross-origin

Quick diagnosis: open Network tab, find failing request, check response headers. If no ACAO header, server problem. If OPTIONS returns 404/405, server not handling preflight. Paste exact error message for sharper answer.
